adai-with-avial.name: Adai with Avial
cooking_steps: Heat a tawa on medium heat and spread 130 ml batter into a thick round.
Drizzle oil around the edge and cook 3 minutes until the underside browns and the top loses its raw sheen.
Flip and cook 2 more minutes on medium-low heat until the center is cooked through.
Serve hot with light avial.

amaranth-dal-with-brown-rice.name: Amaranth Dal with Brown Rice
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a pot over medium heat. Add cumin and garlic and cook 30 seconds until fragrant but not dark.
Add onion, tomato, turmeric, and vegetable and cook 4 minutes until the tomato softens.
Add the dal, salt, and 500 ml water. Pressure cook for 3 whistles or simmer covered on low heat for 25 minutes until the dal is soft and spoon-coating.
Whisk lightly to break some of the dal while keeping some texture, then serve with the cooked grain.

ash-gourd-mor-kuzhambu-with-red-rice.name: Ash Gourd Mor Kuzhambu with Red Rice
cooking_steps: Simmer the vegetable in 250 ml water on medium heat for 6 to 8 minutes until barely tender.
Add the ground paste and cook on low heat for 4 minutes, stirring often so the paste loses its raw edge.
Lower the heat fully and pour in the whisked curd mixture. Heat gently for 3 to 4 minutes, stirring constantly, until steam rises and tiny bubbles appear at the edge; do not boil.
Temper mustard, fenugreek, and curry leaves in oil and pour over the mor kuzhambu. Serve immediately with the cooked grain.

aval-vegetable-upma.name: Aval Vegetable Upma
cooking_steps: Set a kadai over medium heat and dry-roast the main grain for 3 to 4 minutes until nutty and no longer smells raw. Remove to a plate.

Add oil, mustard seeds, chana dal, and urad dal; cook on medium heat for 1 minute until the dals turn pale golden. Add curry leaves, ginger, chilli, onion, and vegetables and saute for 3 minutes until the onion turns translucent.
Pour in water, salt, and bring to a brisk boil over high heat. Rain in the roasted grain while stirring steadily to avoid lumps.
Reduce to low heat, cover, and cook 6 to 8 minutes until the liquid is absorbed and the grains are tender but separate. Rest 3 minutes before fluffing.

avarakkai-paruppu-usili-with-red-rice.name: Avarakkai Paruppu Usili with Red Rice
cooking_steps: Steam or saute the vegetable with a splash of water on medium heat for 5 to 6 minutes until just tender.
Heat oil in a kadai, add mustard and curry leaves, then add the crumbled steamed dal mixture and cook on medium-low heat for 4 minutes until dry and grainy.
Fold in the cooked vegetable and cook 2 minutes more so the flavors combine.
Serve the usili alongside or over the cooked grain as a composed lunch bowl.

banana-stem-mor-kootu-with-kodo-millet.name: Banana Stem Mor Kootu with Kodo Millet
cooking_steps: Simmer the vegetable in 250 ml water on medium heat for 6 to 8 minutes until barely tender.
Add the ground paste and cook on low heat for 4 minutes, stirring often so the paste loses its raw edge.
Lower the heat fully and pour in the whisked curd mixture. Heat gently for 3 to 4 minutes, stirring constantly, until steam rises and tiny bubbles appear at the edge; do not boil.
Temper mustard, fenugreek, and curry leaves in oil and pour over the mor kuzhambu. Serve immediately with the cooked grain.

barnyard-millet-curd-sevai.name: Barnyard Millet Curd Sevai
cooking_steps: Heat a wide pan on medium heat with oil. Add mustard, urad dal, curry leaves, and chilli and cook 45 seconds.
Add onion and vegetable and saute 3 minutes until just tender.
Add the sevai, salt, and 30 ml water and toss on low heat for 2 minutes.
Finish with coconut, toss once, and serve immediately.

barnyard-millet-kozhukattai.name: Barnyard Millet Kozhukattai
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a saute pan on medium heat. Add mustard seeds and urad dal and cook until the dal is lightly golden. Stir in curry leaves, ginger, chilli, and the vegetable; cook 2 minutes.
Pour in water and salt and bring to a full boil over high heat. Lower the heat and stir in the grain slowly until it forms a soft mass.
Cover and rest off heat for 5 minutes. When cool enough to handle, shape into small oblong dumplings with damp hands.
Steam on medium heat for 10 to 12 minutes until the surface looks matte and firm. Serve warm with chutney or a thin lentil broth.

beans-paruppu-curry-with-red-rice.name: Beans Paruppu Curry with Red Rice
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a pot over medium heat. Add cumin and garlic and cook 30 seconds until fragrant but not dark.
Add onion, tomato, turmeric, and vegetable and cook 4 minutes until the tomato softens.
Add the dal, salt, and 500 ml water. Pressure cook for 3 whistles or simmer covered on low heat for 25 minutes until the dal is soft and spoon-coating.
Whisk lightly to break some of the dal while keeping some texture, then serve with the cooked grain.

beetroot-kambu-adai.name: Beetroot Kambu Adai
cooking_steps: Heat the tawa over medium heat. Spread 130 ml batter into a medium-thick round, keeping small gaps for steam to escape.
Drizzle 2 ml oil around the perimeter and cook on medium heat for 3 minutes until the edges look dry and the underside turns chestnut brown.
Flip and cook on medium-low heat for 2 more minutes until the center feels firm and the chopped vegetables no longer look raw.
Serve hot with tomato chutney, keeping the adai thicker than dosa so the coarse lentils stay juicy rather than brittle.

beetroot-kootu-with-little-millet.name: Beetroot Kootu with Little Millet
cooking_steps: Cook the vegetable with 250 ml water and salt over medium heat for 8 to 10 minutes until just tender.
Add the cooked legume and the coconut paste and simmer on low heat for 5 minutes, stirring often so the base does not catch.
In a small pan, heat oil on medium heat, crackle mustard and urad dal, then add curry leaves. Pour this tempering over the kootu immediately.
Fold once, rest 2 minutes for the flavors to settle, and serve with the cooked grain.

bitter-gourd-pitlai-with-little-millet.name: Bitter Gourd Pitlai with Little Millet
cooking_steps: Simmer the vegetable in tamarind water and 300 ml fresh water over medium heat for 10 minutes until tender.
Add the cooked toor dal, cooked pulse accent, ground spice paste, and salt. Cook on low heat for 8 minutes until the gravy thickens and the raw roast aroma disappears.
Temper mustard and curry leaves in oil and pour into the pot.
Rest 5 minutes before serving over the grain.

black-chana-sundal-kuzhambu-with-brown-r.name: Black Chana Sundal Kuzhambu with Brown Rice
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a kadai over medium heat. Add mustard, fenugreek, and curry leaves; once aromatic, add onion and cook 4 minutes until lightly colored.
Add tomato, spice powders, and vegetable; cook 3 minutes until the tomato turns jammy and the masala coats the pieces.

Pour in tamarind extract, 350 ml water, salt, and the cooked pulse accent if using. Simmer on medium-low heat for 14 to 16 minutes until the kuzhambu reduces slightly and the oil forms a thin sheen on top.
Rest 5 minutes before serving over the grain so the acidity rounds out.

bottle-gourd-moong-stew-with-kodo-millet.name: Bottle Gourd Moong Stew with Kodo Millet
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a soup pot over medium heat. Add onion, garlic, ginger, cumin, and pepper and cook 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the vegetable, pulse or grain accent, salt, and water. Bring to a boil, then reduce to low heat and simmer 18 minutes until the vegetables are fully tender.
Blend lightly if the soup benefits from body, or leave chunky for a brothier finish.
Adjust seasoning and serve hot as a complete light dinner.

bottle-gourd-oats-pongal.name: Bottle Gourd Oats Pongal
cooking_steps: Dry-roast the moong dal over medium-low heat for 2 minutes until it smells nutty but does not color.
Combine the grain, dal, vegetable, salt, and water in a pressure cooker or heavy pot. Cook until very soft: 4 whistles in a cooker or 22 to 25 minutes covered on low heat after boiling.
In a small pan, heat oil on medium heat, add cumin, pepper, ginger, and curry leaves, and cook for 40 seconds until fragrant.
Fold the tempering into the cooked pongal and beat vigorously with a ladle for 1 minute so the starch loosens into a creamy spoonable texture.

brinjal-gothsu-with-foxtail-millet.name: Brinjal Gothsu with Foxtail Millet
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a kadai over medium heat. Add mustard, fenugreek, and curry leaves; once aromatic, add onion and cook 4 minutes until lightly colored.
Add tomato, spice powders, and vegetable; cook 3 minutes until the tomato turns jammy and the masala coats the pieces.

Pour in tamarind extract, 350 ml water, salt, and the cooked pulse accent if using. Simmer on medium-low heat for 14 to 16 minutes until the kuzhambu reduces slightly and the oil forms a thin sheen on top.
Rest 5 minutes before serving over the grain so the acidity rounds out.

broken-wheat-vegetable-pongal.name: Broken Wheat Vegetable Pongal
cooking_steps: Dry-roast the moong dal over medium-low heat for 2 minutes.
Cook the grain, dal, salt, and water together until very soft: 4 whistles or 22 minutes on low heat after boiling.
Temper oil with cumin, pepper, ginger, and curry leaves on medium heat for 40 seconds and fold into the pongal.
Quickly saute or add the vegetable garnish as specified and serve hot.

broken-wheat-ven-pongal.name: Broken Wheat Ven Pongal
cooking_steps: Dry-roast the moong dal over medium-low heat for 2 minutes until it smells nutty but does not color.
Combine the grain, dal, vegetable, salt, and water in a pressure cooker or heavy pot. Cook until very soft: 4 whistles in a cooker or 22 to 25 minutes covered on low heat after boiling.
In a small pan, heat oil on medium heat, add cumin, pepper, ginger, and curry leaves, and cook for 40 seconds until fragrant.
Fold the tempering into the cooked pongal and beat vigorously with a ladle for 1 minute so the starch loosens into a creamy spoonable texture.

brown-rice-appam-with-tomato-kurma.name: Brown Rice Appam with Tomato Kurma
cooking_steps: Cook the appam one at a time in an appachatti, swirling the batter for lacy edges and a thicker center.
For the stew, soften onion, ginger, chilli, and curry leaves in oil over medium heat for 2 minutes.
Add the vegetable, peas, salt, and 180 ml water; cover and cook on medium-low heat for 7 minutes until almost tender.
Add coconut milk and simmer gently on low heat for 4 minutes. Serve the stew with hot appam.

brown-rice-appam.name: Brown Rice Appam
cooking_steps: Heat an appachatti over medium heat and grease it with a few drops of oil.
Pour 120 ml batter into the center, lift the pan immediately, and swirl so the batter coats the sides thinly while a thicker pool remains in the middle.
Scatter the vegetable into the center only, cover, and cook on medium-low heat for 2.5 to 3 minutes until the edges lace and the middle looks set but springy.
Do not flip. Remove with a flat spatula and serve hot with tomato chutney.

brown-rice-puttu-with-green-gram.name: Brown Rice Puttu with Green Gram
cooking_steps: Fill the puttu cylinder in alternating layers of grated coconut, damp flour, and cooked green gram, finishing with a spoon of coconut.
Steam over medium heat for 6 to 8 minutes until a strong column of steam escapes from the top and the puttu smells fully cooked.
Rest 1 minute, then push out gently onto a plate so the cylinder stays intact.
Serve warm, breaking it up lightly with a fork before eating.

brown-rice-vegetable-sevai.name: Brown Rice Vegetable Sevai
cooking_steps: Heat a wide pan on medium heat with oil. Add mustard, urad dal, curry leaves, and chilli and cook 45 seconds.
Add onion and vegetable and saute 3 minutes until just tender.
Add the sevai, salt, and 30 ml water and toss on low heat for 2 minutes.
Finish with coconut, toss once, and serve immediately.

In a small pan, heat oil on medium heat, crackle mustard and urad dal, then add curry leaves. Pour this tempering over the kootu immediately.

cooking_steps: Heat a tawa on medium heat and spread 130 ml batter into a thick round.
Drizzle oil around the edge and cook 3 minutes until the underside browns and the top loses its raw sheen.
Flip and cook 2 more minutes on medium-low heat until the center is cooked through.
Serve hot with coriander chutney.

cabbage-moong-dal-adai.name: Cabbage Moong Dal Adai
cooking_steps: Heat the tawa over medium heat. Spread 130 ml batter into a medium-thick round, keeping small gaps for steam to escape.
Drizzle 2 ml oil around the perimeter and cook on medium heat for 3 minutes until the edges look dry and the underside turns chestnut brown.
Flip and cook on medium-low heat for 2 more minutes until the center feels firm and the chopped vegetables no longer look raw.
Serve hot with tomato chutney, keeping the adai thicker than dosa so the coarse lentils stay juicy rather than brittle.

cooking_steps: Heat oil in a kadai over medium heat. Add mustard, fenugreek, and curry leaves; once aromatic, add onion and cook 4 minutes until lightly colored.
Add tomato, spice powders, and vegetable; cook 3 minutes until the tomato turns jammy and the masala coats the pieces.

Pour in tamarind extract, 350 ml water, salt, and the cooked pulse accent if using. Simmer on medium-low heat for 14 to 16 minutes until the kuzhambu reduces slightly and the oil forms a thin sheen on top.
Rest 5 minutes before serving over the grain so the acidity rounds out.

carrot-coconut-idli.name: Carrot Coconut Idli
cooking_steps: Grease the idli moulds very lightly with oil and fill each cavity to three-quarters full.
Steam over medium heat for 10 to 12 minutes until the tops look dry and a skewer comes out clean.
Rest the moulds off heat for 2 minutes so the crumb firms up, then unmould with a wet spoon.
Serve hot with tomato chutney; if holding for later, cool on a rack so steam does not make the idlis gummy.

carrot-peas-sambar-with-barnyard-millet.name: Carrot Peas Sambar with Barnyard Millet
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a pot on medium heat. Add mustard and curry leaves; once crackling, add onion and cook 3 minutes until translucent.
Add tomato, turmeric, sambar powder, and the vegetable; cook 3 minutes until the tomato softens and the spice loses its raw edge.
Pour in tamarind water and 450 ml fresh water. Simmer on medium heat for 10 minutes until the vegetable is tender but intact.
Whisk in the cooked dal and simmer another 6 minutes on low heat until the broth thickens slightly and coats a spoon. Serve over the cooked grain.

cauliflower-milagu-kootu-with-foxtail-mi.name: Cauliflower Milagu Kootu with Foxtail Millet
cooking_steps: Cook the vegetable with 250 ml water and salt over medium heat for 8 to 10 minutes until just tender.
Add the cooked legume and the coconut paste and simmer on low heat for 5 minutes, stirring often so the base does not catch.
In a small pan, heat oil on medium heat, crackle mustard and urad dal, then add curry leaves. Pour this tempering over the kootu immediately.
Fold once, rest 2 minutes for the flavors to settle, and serve with the cooked grain.

chayote-coconut-stew-with-red-rice.name: Chayote Coconut Stew with Red Rice
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a pot on medium heat and soften onion, ginger, chilli, and curry leaves for 3 minutes without browning.
Add the vegetable and 250 ml water, cover, and cook on medium-low heat for 8 minutes until almost tender.
Add the cooked pulse accent, coconut milk, and salt and simmer gently on low heat for 5 minutes until the broth lightly coats the spoon.
Serve the stew over or beside the cooked grain.

chow-chow-kootu-with-foxtail-millet.name: Chow Chow Kootu with Foxtail Millet
cooking_steps: Cook the vegetable with 250 ml water and salt over medium heat for 8 to 10 minutes until just tender.
Add the cooked legume and the coconut paste and simmer on low heat for 5 minutes, stirring often so the base does not catch.
In a small pan, heat oil on medium heat, crackle mustard and urad dal, then add curry leaves. Pour this tempering over the kootu immediately.
Fold once, rest 2 minutes for the flavors to settle, and serve with the cooked grain.

Pour 120 ml batter into the center, lift the pan immediately, and swirl so the batter coats the sides thinly while a thicker pool remains in the middle.
Scatter the vegetable into the center only, cover, and cook on medium-low heat for 2.5 to 3 minutes until the edges lace and the middle looks set but springy.

coconut-vegetable-soup-with-ragi-string-.name: Coconut Vegetable Soup with Ragi String Hoppers
cooking_steps: Warm a pot on medium heat with oil. Add onion, ginger, chilli, and curry leaves and soften for 2 minutes without browning.
Add the vegetable, peas, salt, and 180 ml water. Cover and cook on medium-low heat for 6 minutes until just tender.
Add coconut milk and simmer gently on low heat for 4 minutes until lightly thickened.
Serve the hot stew over the idiyappam so the strands absorb flavor but still retain shape.

corn-cowpea-kuzhi-paniyaram.name: Corn Cowpea Kuzhi Paniyaram
cooking_steps: Heat the paniyaram pan over medium heat for 2 minutes and brush each cavity lightly with oil.
Mix onion, vegetable, ginger, chilli, curry leaves, and salt into the batter. Fill each cavity three-quarters full.
Cook covered on medium-low heat for 3 minutes until the edges set and the bottom turns deep golden.
Turn each paniyaram with a skewer, cook 2 more minutes until evenly browned and springy, then serve hot.

cucumber-buttermilk-kichadi-with-foxtail.name: Cucumber Buttermilk Kichadi with Foxtail Millet
cooking_steps: Combine the grain, moong dal, vegetable, salt, and water in a pot and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce to low heat and cook covered for 18 to 20 minutes, stirring twice, until the mixture turns soft and porridge-like.
Heat oil in a small pan, add cumin, pepper, and ginger, and cook on medium heat for 30 seconds. Stir into the kanji.
Cool for 2 minutes, then fold in the buttermilk for a softer finish before serving.

cucumber-millet-kichadi.name: Cucumber Millet Kichadi
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a cooker or heavy pot on medium heat. Add mustard, cumin, and curry leaves; once aromatic, add onion, ginger, chilli, and vegetables and cook 3 minutes.
Add the grain, dal, salt, and water and bring to a boil over high heat.
Pressure cook for 3 whistles or cover and cook on low heat for 18 to 20 minutes until the grain is soft and spoonable.
Rest 5 minutes, then stir briskly once to unify the starch and vegetables before serving.

curry-leaf-kuzhambu-with-red-rice.name: Curry Leaf Kuzhambu with Red Rice
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a kadai over medium heat. Add mustard, fenugreek, and curry leaves; once aromatic, add onion and cook 4 minutes until lightly colored.
Add tomato, spice powders, and vegetable; cook 3 minutes until the tomato turns jammy and the masala coats the pieces.

Pour in tamarind extract, 350 ml water, salt, and the cooked pulse accent if using. Simmer on medium-low heat for 14 to 16 minutes until the kuzhambu reduces slightly and the oil forms a thin sheen on top.
Rest 5 minutes before serving over the grain so the acidity rounds out.

curry-leaf-pepper-rasam-with-red-rice.name: Curry Leaf Pepper Rasam with Red Rice
cooking_steps: Combine tamarind water, tomato, vegetable element, salt, and 500 ml water in a pot and simmer on medium heat for 8 minutes.
Add the cooked pulse and the crushed pepper-cumin-garlic mixture and simmer 4 minutes more until the raw garlic smell disappears.
Temper mustard and curry leaves in oil over medium heat and pour over the rasam. Switch off the heat as soon as the rasam foams lightly at the edges.
Rest 2 minutes, then ladle over the cooked grain.

curry-leaf-pesarattu.name: Curry Leaf Pesarattu
cooking_steps: Heat the tawa on medium-high heat and wipe it with a thin film of oil.
Pour 120 ml batter and spread it quickly into a thin round before the surface sets. Scatter the onion mixture evenly and press lightly with the back of the ladle.
Cook on medium heat for 2.5 to 3 minutes until the edges lift easily and the base turns mottled brown. Drizzle 2 ml oil along the edge.
Flip for 45 seconds only if needed; the second side should stay soft. Serve immediately with tomato chutney.

curry-leaf-podi-sevai.name: Curry Leaf Podi Sevai
cooking_steps: Heat a wide pan on medium heat, add oil, mustard seeds, urad dal, and cook 45 seconds until the dal turns pale gold.
Add curry leaves, ginger, chilli, onion, and vegetables; saute 3 to 4 minutes until just tender but still bright.
Add the sevai, salt, and 40 ml water. Toss gently on low heat for 2 minutes so the strands absorb seasoning without breaking.
Finish with grated coconut and toss once more. Rest 2 minutes before serving so the starch firms and the strands stay separate.

curry-leaf-thinai-dosa.name: Curry Leaf Thinai Dosa
cooking_steps: Heat a cast-iron dosa tawa over medium heat for 3 minutes; sprinkle a few drops of water and wipe when it sizzles and vanishes immediately.

Pour 120 ml batter in the center, spread outward in a thin spiral, and drizzle 2 ml oil around the edges. Cook on medium heat for 2 to 3 minutes until the surface dries and the base turns deep golden.
Flip only if the topping looks moist; cook the second side on low heat for 45 to 60 seconds until set but not crisp.
Repeat with the remaining batter, adjusting the heat so each dosa browns gradually rather than blistering. Serve hot with tomato chutney.

curry-leaf-thuvaiyal-rice-bowl.name: Curry Leaf Thuvaiyal Rice Bowl
cooking_steps: Roast the pulse accent and chillies in a small pan on medium-low heat until fragrant. Add the vegetable if it benefits from wilting or softening and cook until concentrated.
Grind with tamarind, coconut, salt, and a little water into a thick paste.
Warm the paste briefly in the pan with 1 tsp oil if you want a mellower flavor, then toss through the cooked grain or serve it in a thick layer over the grain bowl.
Let the bowl stand 2 minutes before eating so the paste seasons the grain evenly.

drumstick-leaf-adai.name: Drumstick Leaf Adai

cooking_steps: Grease the idli moulds very lightly with oil and fill each cavity to three-quarters full.

Rest the moulds off heat for 2 minutes so the crumb firms up, then unmould with a wet spoon.

foxtail-millet-pidi-kozhukattai-in-peppe.name: Foxtail Millet Pidi Kozhukattai in Pepper Broth
cooking_steps: Steam the shaped dumplings on medium heat for 10 minutes until firm and matte.
In a pot, warm oil and saute ginger, chilli, curry leaves, and the vegetable for 2 minutes on medium heat.
Add cooked moong dal, water, coconut milk, and salt and simmer on low heat for 5 minutes.
Slide in the steamed dumplings and cook 2 minutes more so they absorb some broth before serving.

Heat oil in a small pan, add cumin, pepper, and ginger, and cook on medium heat for 30 seconds. Stir into the kanji.

kambu-roti-with-ridge-gourd-kootu.name: Kambu Roti with Ridge Gourd Kootu
cooking_steps: Cook the vegetable with a splash of water on medium heat until just tender, then add the cooked dal and coconut paste and simmer 5 minutes on low heat.
Temper the kootu with oil and curry leaves.
Divide the dough into 4 balls, roll each into a thin roti, and cook on a hot tawa over medium-high heat for 1 minute per side until brown spots appear.
Serve the rotis hot with the soft kootu.

Repeat with the remaining batter, adjusting the heat so each dosa browns gradually rather than blistering. Serve hot with tomato chutney.

kathirikai-mochai-kuzhambu-with-brown-ri.name: Kathirikai Mochai Kuzhambu with Brown Rice
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a kadai over medium heat. Add mustard, fenugreek, and curry leaves; once aromatic, add onion and cook 4 minutes until lightly colored.
Add tomato, spice powders, and vegetable; cook 3 minutes until the tomato turns jammy and the masala coats the pieces.

Pour in tamarind extract, 350 ml water, salt, and the cooked pulse accent if using. Simmer on medium-low heat for 14 to 16 minutes until the kuzhambu reduces slightly and the oil forms a thin sheen on top.
Rest 5 minutes before serving over the grain so the acidity rounds out.

keerai-adai-with-garlic-chutney.name: Keerai Adai with Garlic Chutney
cooking_steps: Heat a tawa on medium heat and spread 130 ml batter into a thick round.
Drizzle oil around the edge and cook 3 minutes until the underside browns and the top loses its raw sheen.
Flip and cook 2 more minutes on medium-low heat until the center is cooked through.
Serve hot with garlic chutney.

keerai-kootu-with-red-rice.name: Keerai Kootu with Red Rice
cooking_steps: Cook the vegetable with 250 ml water and salt over medium heat for 8 to 10 minutes until just tender.
Add the cooked legume and the coconut paste and simmer on low heat for 5 minutes, stirring often so the base does not catch.
In a small pan, heat oil on medium heat, crackle mustard and urad dal, then add curry leaves. Pour this tempering over the kootu immediately.
Fold once, rest 2 minutes for the flavors to settle, and serve with the cooked grain.

keerai-kuzhambu-with-thinai.name: Keerai Kuzhambu with Thinai
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a kadai over medium heat. Add mustard, fenugreek, and curry leaves; once aromatic, add onion and cook 4 minutes until lightly colored.
Add tomato, spice powders, and vegetable; cook 3 minutes until the tomato turns jammy and the masala coats the pieces.

Pour in tamarind extract, 350 ml water, salt, and the cooked pulse accent if using. Simmer on medium-low heat for 14 to 16 minutes until the kuzhambu reduces slightly and the oil forms a thin sheen on top.
Rest 5 minutes before serving over the grain so the acidity rounds out.

keerai-pesarattu.name: Keerai Pesarattu
cooking_steps: Heat the tawa on medium-high heat and wipe it with a thin film of oil.
Pour 120 ml batter and spread it quickly into a thin round before the surface sets. Scatter the onion mixture evenly and press lightly with the back of the ladle.
Cook on medium heat for 2.5 to 3 minutes until the edges lift easily and the base turns mottled brown. Drizzle 2 ml oil along the edge.
Flip for 45 seconds only if needed; the second side should stay soft. Serve immediately with tomato chutney.

knol-khol-sambar-with-foxtail-millet.name: Knol Khol Sambar with Foxtail Millet
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a pot on medium heat. Add mustard and curry leaves; once crackling, add onion and cook 3 minutes until translucent.
Add tomato, turmeric, sambar powder, and the vegetable; cook 3 minutes until the tomato softens and the spice loses its raw edge.
Pour in tamarind water and 450 ml fresh water. Simmer on medium heat for 10 minutes until the vegetable is tender but intact.
Whisk in the cooked dal and simmer another 6 minutes on low heat until the broth thickens slightly and coats a spoon. Serve over the cooked grain.

knol-khol-sambar-with-little-millet.name: Knol Khol Sambar with Little Millet
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a pot on medium heat. Add mustard and curry leaves; once crackling, add onion and cook 3 minutes until translucent.
Add tomato, turmeric, sambar powder, and the vegetable; cook 3 minutes until the tomato softens and the spice loses its raw edge.
Pour in tamarind water and 450 ml fresh water. Simmer on medium heat for 10 minutes until the vegetable is tender but intact.
Whisk in the cooked dal and simmer another 6 minutes on low heat until the broth thickens slightly and coats a spoon. Serve over the cooked grain.

kollu-adai.name: Kollu Adai
cooking_steps: Heat the tawa over medium heat. Spread 130 ml batter into a medium-thick round, keeping small gaps for steam to escape.
Drizzle 2 ml oil around the perimeter and cook on medium heat for 3 minutes until the edges look dry and the underside turns chestnut brown.
Flip and cook on medium-low heat for 2 more minutes until the center feels firm and the chopped vegetables no longer look raw.
Serve hot with tomato chutney, keeping the adai thicker than dosa so the coarse lentils stay juicy rather than brittle.

kollu-rasam-with-millet-rice.name: Kollu Rasam with Millet Rice
cooking_steps: Combine tamarind water, tomato, vegetable element, salt, and 500 ml water in a pot and simmer on medium heat for 8 minutes.
Add the cooked pulse and the crushed pepper-cumin-garlic mixture and simmer 4 minutes more until the raw garlic smell disappears.
Temper mustard and curry leaves in oil over medium heat and pour over the rasam. Switch off the heat as soon as the rasam foams lightly at the edges.
Rest 2 minutes, then ladle over the cooked grain.

kollu-soup-with-cabbage-thoran.name: Kollu Soup with Cabbage Thoran
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a soup pot over medium heat. Add onion, garlic, ginger, cumin, and pepper and cook 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the vegetable, pulse or grain accent, salt, and water. Bring to a boil, then reduce to low heat and simmer 18 minutes until the vegetables are fully tender.
Blend lightly if the soup benefits from body, or leave chunky for a brothier finish.
Adjust seasoning and serve hot as a complete light dinner.

kovakkai-peanut-kuzhambu-with-brown-rice.name: Kovakkai Peanut Kuzhambu with Brown Rice
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a kadai over medium heat. Add mustard, fenugreek, and curry leaves; once aromatic, add onion and cook 4 minutes until lightly colored.
Add tomato, spice powders, and vegetable; cook 3 minutes until the tomato turns jammy and the masala coats the pieces.

Pour in tamarind extract, 350 ml water, salt, and the cooked pulse accent if using. Simmer on medium-low heat for 14 to 16 minutes until the kuzhambu reduces slightly and the oil forms a thin sheen on top.
Rest 5 minutes before serving over the grain so the acidity rounds out.

kuthiraivali-coconut-sevai.name: Kuthiraivali Coconut Sevai
cooking_steps: Heat a wide pan on medium heat, add oil, mustard seeds, urad dal, and cook 45 seconds until the dal turns pale gold.
Add curry leaves, ginger, chilli, onion, and vegetables; saute 3 to 4 minutes until just tender but still bright.
Add the sevai, salt, and 40 ml water. Toss gently on low heat for 2 minutes so the strands absorb seasoning without breaking.
Finish with grated coconut and toss once more. Rest 2 minutes before serving so the starch firms and the strands stay separate.

lauki-coconut-milk-soup-with-barnyard-mi.name: Lauki Coconut Milk Soup with Barnyard Millet
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a soup pot over medium heat. Add onion, garlic, ginger, cumin, and pepper and cook 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the vegetable, pulse or grain accent, salt, and water. Bring to a boil, then reduce to low heat and simmer 18 minutes until the vegetables are fully tender.
Blend lightly if the soup benefits from body, or leave chunky for a brothier finish.
Adjust seasoning and serve hot as a complete light dinner.

little-millet-upma-kozhukattai.name: Little Millet Upma Kozhukattai
cooking_steps: Steam the shaped dumplings on medium heat for 10 minutes until firm and matte.
In a pot, warm oil and saute ginger, chilli, curry leaves, and the vegetable for 2 minutes on medium heat.
Add cooked moong dal, water, coconut milk, and salt and simmer on low heat for 5 minutes.
Slide in the steamed dumplings and cook 2 minutes more so they absorb some broth before serving.

little-millet-vegetable-upma.name: Little Millet Vegetable Upma
cooking_steps: Set a kadai over medium heat and dry-roast the main grain for 3 to 4 minutes until nutty and no longer smells raw. Remove to a plate.

Add oil, mustard seeds, chana dal, and urad dal; cook on medium heat for 1 minute until the dals turn pale golden. Add curry leaves, ginger, chilli, onion, and vegetables and saute for 3 minutes until the onion turns translucent.
Pour in water, salt, and bring to a brisk boil over high heat. Rain in the roasted grain while stirring steadily to avoid lumps.
Reduce to low heat, cover, and cook 6 to 8 minutes until the liquid is absorbed and the grains are tender but separate. Rest 3 minutes before fluffing.

manathakkali-style-kuzhambu-with-red-ric.name: Manathakkali Style Kuzhambu with Red Rice
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a kadai over medium heat. Add mustard, fenugreek, and curry leaves; once aromatic, add onion and cook 4 minutes until lightly colored.

Rest 5 minutes before serving over the grain so the acidity rounds out.

masala-vegetable-oats-uttappam.name: Masala Vegetable Oats Uttappam
cooking_steps: Warm the tawa on medium heat for 2 minutes and grease it lightly with 1 ml oil.
Pour 140 ml batter without spreading it too thin; nudge it only enough to form a 12 to 14 cm round. Scatter one-quarter of the topping mix over it and press gently.
Drizzle 2 ml oil around the edge and cook on medium-low heat for 3 minutes until the sides look dry and the underside turns evenly golden.
Flip carefully and cook on low heat for 1 to 1.5 minutes until the vegetables soften and faint brown patches appear. Serve immediately.

methi-kuzhambu-with-brown-rice.name: Methi Kuzhambu with Brown Rice

cooking_steps: Heat a seasoned tawa on medium-high heat for 2 minutes.
Pour 120 ml batter and spread thinly. Scatter the vegetable and onion if using, then drizzle a little oil around the edge.
Cook 2 to 3 minutes on medium heat until the base is speckled brown and the top is dry.
Flip briefly if needed and serve immediately with capsicum chutney.

cooking_steps: Warm the tawa on medium heat for 2 minutes and grease it lightly with 1 ml oil.

Flip carefully and cook on low heat for 1 to 1.5 minutes until the vegetables soften and faint brown patches appear. Serve immediately.

cooking_steps: Set a kadai over medium heat and dry-roast the main grain for 3 to 4 minutes until nutty and no longer smells raw. Remove to a plate.

Add oil, mustard seeds, chana dal, and urad dal; cook on medium heat for 1 minute until the dals turn pale golden. Add curry leaves, ginger, chilli, onion, and vegetables and saute for 3 minutes until the onion turns translucent.
Pour in water, salt, and bring to a brisk boil over high heat. Rain in the roasted grain while stirring steadily to avoid lumps.

millet-pidi-kozhukattai-with-lentil-stew.name: Millet Pidi Kozhukattai with Lentil Stew

millet-pongal-with-pepper-mushroom.name: Millet Pongal with Pepper Mushroom
cooking_steps: Dry-roast the moong dal over medium-low heat for 2 minutes.
Cook the grain, dal, salt, and water together until very soft: 4 whistles or 22 minutes on low heat after boiling.
Temper oil with cumin, pepper, ginger, and curry leaves on medium heat for 40 seconds and fold into the pongal.
Quickly saute or add the vegetable garnish as specified and serve hot.

mixed-vegetable-sambar-sadam.name: Mixed Vegetable Sambar Sadam
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a pot on medium heat. Add mustard and curry leaves; once crackling, add onion and cook 3 minutes until translucent.
Add tomato, turmeric, sambar powder, and the vegetable; cook 3 minutes until the tomato softens and the spice loses its raw edge.
Pour in tamarind water and 450 ml fresh water. Simmer on medium heat for 10 minutes until the vegetable is tender but intact.
Whisk in the cooked dal and simmer another 6 minutes on low heat until the broth thickens slightly and coats a spoon. Serve over the cooked grain.

moong-dal-dosa-with-tomato-chutney.name: Moong Dal Dosa with Tomato Chutney
cooking_steps: Heat a seasoned tawa on medium-high heat for 2 minutes.
Pour 120 ml batter and spread thinly. Scatter the vegetable and onion if using, then drizzle a little oil around the edge.
Cook 2 to 3 minutes on medium heat until the base is speckled brown and the top is dry.
Flip briefly if needed and serve immediately with tomato chutney.

moringa-leaf-dal-with-barnyard-millet.name: Moringa Leaf Dal with Barnyard Millet
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a pot over medium heat. Add cumin and garlic and cook 30 seconds until fragrant but not dark.
Add onion, tomato, turmeric, and vegetable and cook 4 minutes until the tomato softens.
Add the dal, salt, and 500 ml water. Pressure cook for 3 whistles or simmer covered on low heat for 25 minutes until the dal is soft and spoon-coating.
Whisk lightly to break some of the dal while keeping some texture, then serve with the cooked grain.

moringa-leaf-dosa.name: Moringa Leaf Dosa
cooking_steps: Heat a cast-iron dosa tawa over medium heat for 3 minutes; sprinkle a few drops of water and wipe when it sizzles and vanishes immediately.

Pour 120 ml batter in the center, spread outward in a thin spiral, and drizzle 2 ml oil around the edges. Cook on medium heat for 2 to 3 minutes until the surface dries and the base turns deep golden.
Flip only if the topping looks moist; cook the second side on low heat for 45 to 60 seconds until set but not crisp.
Repeat with the remaining batter, adjusting the heat so each dosa browns gradually rather than blistering. Serve hot with tomato chutney.

multi-millet-adai.name: Multi Millet Adai

cooking_steps: Heat a tawa on medium heat and grease lightly.
Pour a ladle of batter into a thick round, scatter the vegetables over the top, and press gently.
Cook 3 minutes on medium-low heat until the bottom turns golden and the top starts to set.
Flip and cook 1 minute on low heat, then serve with onion chutney.

oats-and-red-rice-idli-with-sambar.name: Oats and Red Rice Idli with Sambar
cooking_steps: Steam the idlis on medium heat for 10 to 12 minutes until springy and fully set.
For the saaru, simmer dal, tomato, vegetable, garlic, cumin, pepper, salt, and 400 ml water over medium heat for 10 minutes until fragrant and lightly broth-like.
Temper with a little oil if desired and pour the hot saaru into bowls.
Serve the idlis alongside or submerged briefly so they soak up the broth.

oats-kuzhi-paniyaram.name: Oats Kuzhi Paniyaram
cooking_steps: Heat the paniyaram pan over medium heat for 2 minutes and brush each cavity lightly with oil.
Mix onion, vegetable, ginger, chilli, curry leaves, and salt into the batter. Fill each cavity three-quarters full.
Cook covered on medium-low heat for 3 minutes until the edges set and the bottom turns deep golden.
Turn each paniyaram with a skewer, cook 2 more minutes until evenly browned and springy, then serve hot.

onion-tomato-kuthiraivali-uthappam.name: Onion Tomato Kuthiraivali Uthappam

pearl-millet-puttu.name: Pearl Millet Puttu
cooking_steps: Fill the puttu cylinder in alternating layers of grated coconut, damp flour, and cooked green gram, finishing with a spoon of coconut.
Steam over medium heat for 6 to 8 minutes until a strong column of steam escapes from the top and the puttu smells fully cooked.
Rest 1 minute, then push out gently onto a plate so the cylinder stays intact.
Serve warm, breaking it up lightly with a fork before eating.

pepper-jeera-thinai-kanji.name: Pepper Jeera Thinai Kanji
cooking_steps: Combine the grain, moong dal, vegetable, salt, and water in a pot and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce to low heat and cook covered for 18 to 20 minutes, stirring twice, until the mixture turns soft and porridge-like.
Heat oil in a small pan, add cumin, pepper, and ginger, and cook on medium heat for 30 seconds. Stir into the kanji.
Cool for 2 minutes, then fold in the buttermilk for a softer finish before serving.

pumpkin-idli.name: Pumpkin Idli
cooking_steps: Grease the idli moulds very lightly with oil and fill each cavity to three-quarters full.
Steam over medium heat for 10 to 12 minutes until the tops look dry and a skewer comes out clean.
Rest the moulds off heat for 2 minutes so the crumb firms up, then unmould with a wet spoon.
Serve hot with tomato chutney; if holding for later, cool on a rack so steam does not make the idlis gummy.

pumpkin-more-kuzhambu-with-barnyard-mill.name: Pumpkin More Kuzhambu with Barnyard Millet
cooking_steps: Simmer the vegetable in 250 ml water on medium heat for 6 to 8 minutes until barely tender.
Add the ground paste and cook on low heat for 4 minutes, stirring often so the paste loses its raw edge.
Lower the heat fully and pour in the whisked curd mixture. Heat gently for 3 to 4 minutes, stirring constantly, until steam rises and tiny bubbles appear at the edge; do not boil.
Temper mustard, fenugreek, and curry leaves in oil and pour over the mor kuzhambu. Serve immediately with the cooked grain.

pumpkin-soup-with-foxtail-millet-dumplin.name: Pumpkin Soup with Foxtail Millet Dumplings

cooking_steps: Dry-roast the moong dal over medium-low heat for 2 minutes until it smells nutty but does not color.
Combine the grain, dal, vegetable, salt, and water in a pressure cooker or heavy pot. Cook until very soft: 4 whistles in a cooker or 22 to 25 minutes covered on low heat after boiling.
In a small pan, heat oil on medium heat, add cumin, pepper, ginger, and curry leaves, and cook for 40 seconds until fragrant.
Fold the tempering into the cooked pongal and beat vigorously with a ladle for 1 minute so the starch loosens into a creamy spoonable texture.

ragi-dosa.name: Ragi Dosa
cooking_steps: Heat a cast-iron dosa tawa over medium heat for 3 minutes; sprinkle a few drops of water and wipe when it sizzles and vanishes immediately.

ragi-mudde-with-keerai-saaru.name: Ragi Mudde with Keerai Saaru
cooking_steps: Bring 360 ml water with salt to a rolling boil over high heat. Rain in the flour while stirring briskly; lower to medium-low and beat for 5 to 6 minutes until thick and glossy.
With wet hands, shape the hot cooked dough into 4 compact balls once cool enough to handle.
For the saaru, simmer dal, vegetable, tomato, garlic, cumin, pepper, and 260 ml water for 10 minutes on medium heat until lightly broth-like.
Serve the mudde hot with the saaru poured alongside or over torn pieces.

ragi-semiya-upma.name: Ragi Semiya Upma
cooking_steps: Heat a wide pan on medium heat with oil. Add mustard, urad dal, curry leaves, and chilli and cook 45 seconds.
Add onion and vegetable and saute 3 minutes until just tender.
Add the sevai, salt, and 30 ml water and toss on low heat for 2 minutes.
Finish with coconut, toss once, and serve immediately.

raw-banana-adai-with-curd-dip.name: Raw Banana Adai with Curd Dip
cooking_steps: Heat a tawa on medium heat and spread 130 ml batter into a thick round.
Drizzle oil around the edge and cook 3 minutes until the underside browns and the top loses its raw sheen.
Flip and cook 2 more minutes on medium-low heat until the center is cooked through.
Serve hot with spiced low-fat curd.

raw-mango-kuzhambu-with-little-millet.name: Raw Mango Kuzhambu with Little Millet
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a kadai over medium heat. Add mustard, fenugreek, and curry leaves; once aromatic, add onion and cook 4 minutes until lightly colored.
Add tomato, spice powders, and vegetable; cook 3 minutes until the tomato turns jammy and the masala coats the pieces.

Pour in tamarind extract, 350 ml water, salt, and the cooked pulse accent if using. Simmer on medium-low heat for 14 to 16 minutes until the kuzhambu reduces slightly and the oil forms a thin sheen on top.
Rest 5 minutes before serving over the grain so the acidity rounds out.

red-aval-vegetable-upma.name: Red Aval Vegetable Upma
cooking_steps: Set a kadai over medium heat and dry-roast the main grain for 3 to 4 minutes until nutty and no longer smells raw. Remove to a plate.

Add oil, mustard seeds, chana dal, and urad dal; cook on medium heat for 1 minute until the dals turn pale golden. Add curry leaves, ginger, chilli, onion, and vegetables and saute for 3 minutes until the onion turns translucent.
Pour in water, salt, and bring to a brisk boil over high heat. Rain in the roasted grain while stirring steadily to avoid lumps.
Reduce to low heat, cover, and cook 6 to 8 minutes until the liquid is absorbed and the grains are tender but separate. Rest 3 minutes before fluffing.

red-rice-aval-upma.name: Red Rice Aval Upma
cooking_steps: Set a kadai over medium heat and dry-roast the main grain for 3 to 4 minutes until nutty and no longer smells raw. Remove to a plate.

Add oil, mustard seeds, chana dal, and urad dal; cook on medium heat for 1 minute until the dals turn pale golden. Add curry leaves, ginger, chilli, onion, and vegetables and saute for 3 minutes until the onion turns translucent.
Pour in water, salt, and bring to a brisk boil over high heat. Rain in the roasted grain while stirring steadily to avoid lumps.
Reduce to low heat, cover, and cook 6 to 8 minutes until the liquid is absorbed and the grains are tender but separate. Rest 3 minutes before fluffing.

red-rice-idli.name: Red Rice Idli
cooking_steps: Grease the idli moulds very lightly with oil and fill each cavity to three-quarters full.
Steam over medium heat for 10 to 12 minutes until the tops look dry and a skewer comes out clean.
Rest the moulds off heat for 2 minutes so the crumb firms up, then unmould with a wet spoon.
Serve hot with tomato chutney; if holding for later, cool on a rack so steam does not make the idlis gummy.

red-rice-neer-dosa-with-vegetable-saagu.name: Red Rice Neer Dosa with Vegetable Saagu
cooking_steps: Heat a seasoned tawa on medium-high heat for 2 minutes.
Pour 120 ml batter and spread thinly. Scatter the vegetable and onion if using, then drizzle a little oil around the edge.
Cook 2 to 3 minutes on medium heat until the base is speckled brown and the top is dry.
Flip briefly if needed and serve immediately with vegetable saagu.

ridge-gourd-adai.name: Ridge Gourd Adai
cooking_steps: Heat the tawa over medium heat. Spread 130 ml batter into a medium-thick round, keeping small gaps for steam to escape.
Drizzle 2 ml oil around the perimeter and cook on medium heat for 3 minutes until the edges look dry and the underside turns chestnut brown.
Flip and cook on medium-low heat for 2 more minutes until the center feels firm and the chopped vegetables no longer look raw.
Serve hot with tomato chutney, keeping the adai thicker than dosa so the coarse lentils stay juicy rather than brittle.

ridge-gourd-dal-kuzhambu-with-kodo-mille.name: Ridge Gourd Dal Kuzhambu with Kodo Millet
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a pot over medium heat. Add cumin and garlic and cook 30 seconds until fragrant but not dark.
Add onion, tomato, turmeric, and vegetable and cook 4 minutes until the tomato softens.
Add the dal, salt, and 500 ml water. Pressure cook for 3 whistles or simmer covered on low heat for 25 minutes until the dal is soft and spoon-coating.
Whisk lightly to break some of the dal while keeping some texture, then serve with the cooked grain.

saamai-vegetable-kichadi.name: Saamai Vegetable Kichadi
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a cooker or heavy pot on medium heat. Add mustard, cumin, and curry leaves; once aromatic, add onion, ginger, chilli, and vegetables and cook 3 minutes.
Add the grain, dal, salt, and water and bring to a boil over high heat.
Pressure cook for 3 whistles or cover and cook on low heat for 18 to 20 minutes until the grain is soft and spoonable.
Rest 5 minutes, then stir briskly once to unify the starch and vegetables before serving.

samai-vegetable-pongal.name: Samai Vegetable Pongal
cooking_steps: Dry-roast the moong dal over medium-low heat for 2 minutes until it smells nutty but does not color.
Combine the grain, dal, vegetable, salt, and water in a pressure cooker or heavy pot. Cook until very soft: 4 whistles in a cooker or 22 to 25 minutes covered on low heat after boiling.
In a small pan, heat oil on medium heat, add cumin, pepper, ginger, and curry leaves, and cook for 40 seconds until fragrant.
Fold the tempering into the cooked pongal and beat vigorously with a ladle for 1 minute so the starch loosens into a creamy spoonable texture.

samai-vegetable-uttappam.name: Samai Vegetable Uttappam
cooking_steps: Heat a tawa on medium heat and grease lightly.
Pour a ladle of batter into a thick round, scatter the vegetables over the top, and press gently.
Cook 3 minutes on medium-low heat until the bottom turns golden and the top starts to set.
Flip and cook 1 minute on low heat, then serve with tomato chutney.

set-dosa-with-green-pea-kurma.name: Set Dosa with Green Pea Kurma
cooking_steps: Heat a seasoned tawa on medium-high heat for 2 minutes.
Pour 120 ml batter and spread thinly. Scatter the vegetable and onion if using, then drizzle a little oil around the edge.
Cook 2 to 3 minutes on medium heat until the base is speckled brown and the top is dry.
Flip briefly if needed and serve immediately with green pea kurma.

snake-gourd-poricha-kootu-with-red-rice.name: Snake Gourd Poricha Kootu with Red Rice
cooking_steps: Cook the vegetable with 250 ml water and salt over medium heat for 8 to 10 minutes until just tender.
Add the cooked legume and the coconut paste and simmer on low heat for 5 minutes, stirring often so the base does not catch.
In a small pan, heat oil on medium heat, crackle mustard and urad dal, then add curry leaves. Pour this tempering over the kootu immediately.
Fold once, rest 2 minutes for the flavors to settle, and serve with the cooked grain.

sorakkai-kootu-with-brown-rice.name: Sorakkai Kootu with Brown Rice
cooking_steps: Cook the vegetable with 250 ml water and salt over medium heat for 8 to 10 minutes until just tender.
Add the cooked legume and the coconut paste and simmer on low heat for 5 minutes, stirring often so the base does not catch.
In a small pan, heat oil on medium heat, crackle mustard and urad dal, then add curry leaves. Pour this tempering over the kootu immediately.
Fold once, rest 2 minutes for the flavors to settle, and serve with the cooked grain.

south-indian-sundal-salad-bowl.name: South Indian Sundal Salad Bowl
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a soup pot over medium heat. Add onion, garlic, ginger, cumin, and pepper and cook 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the vegetable, pulse or grain accent, salt, and water. Bring to a boil, then reduce to low heat and simmer 18 minutes until the vegetables are fully tender.
Blend lightly if the soup benefits from body, or leave chunky for a brothier finish.
Adjust seasoning and serve hot as a complete light dinner.

spinach-pesarattu-rolls.name: Spinach Pesarattu Rolls
cooking_steps: Heat a seasoned tawa on medium-high heat for 2 minutes.
Pour 120 ml batter and spread thinly. Scatter the vegetable and onion if using, then drizzle a little oil around the edge.
Cook 2 to 3 minutes on medium heat until the base is speckled brown and the top is dry.
Flip briefly if needed and serve immediately with ginger chutney.

sundakkai-style-kuzhambu-with-barnyard-m.name: Sundakkai Style Kuzhambu with Barnyard Millet

thakkali-pappu-with-foxtail-millet.name: Thakkali Pappu with Foxtail Millet
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a pot over medium heat. Add cumin and garlic and cook 30 seconds until fragrant but not dark.

thinai-coconut-puttu.name: Thinai Coconut Puttu
cooking_steps: Fill the puttu cylinder in alternating layers of grated coconut, damp flour, and cooked green gram, finishing with a spoon of coconut.
Steam over medium heat for 6 to 8 minutes until a strong column of steam escapes from the top and the puttu smells fully cooked.
Rest 1 minute, then push out gently onto a plate so the cylinder stays intact.
Serve warm, breaking it up lightly with a fork before eating.

thinai-onion-uthappam.name: Thinai Onion Uthappam
cooking_steps: Warm the tawa on medium heat for 2 minutes and grease it lightly with 1 ml oil.
Pour 140 ml batter without spreading it too thin; nudge it only enough to form a 12 to 14 cm round. Scatter one-quarter of the topping mix over it and press gently.
Drizzle 2 ml oil around the edge and cook on medium-low heat for 3 minutes until the sides look dry and the underside turns evenly golden.
Flip carefully and cook on low heat for 1 to 1.5 minutes until the vegetables soften and faint brown patches appear. Serve immediately.

thinai-vegetable-kanji.name: Thinai Vegetable Kanji
cooking_steps: Combine the grain, moong dal, vegetable, salt, and water in a pot and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce to low heat and cook covered for 18 to 20 minutes, stirring twice, until the mixture turns soft and porridge-like.
Heat oil in a small pan, add cumin, pepper, and ginger, and cook on medium heat for 30 seconds. Stir into the kanji.
Cool for 2 minutes, then fold in the buttermilk for a softer finish before serving.

tomato-millet-bath.name: Tomato Millet Bath
cooking_steps: Set a kadai over medium heat and dry-roast the main grain for 3 to 4 minutes until nutty and no longer smells raw. Remove to a plate.

tomato-millet-sevai.name: Tomato Millet Sevai

tomato-oats-uttappam.name: Tomato Oats Uttappam

tomato-pepper-rasam-with-horse-gram-rice.name: Tomato Pepper Rasam with Horse Gram Rice
cooking_steps: Combine tamarind water, tomato, vegetable element, salt, and 500 ml water in a pot and simmer on medium heat for 8 minutes.
Add the cooked pulse and the crushed pepper-cumin-garlic mixture and simmer 4 minutes more until the raw garlic smell disappears.
Temper mustard and curry leaves in oil over medium heat and pour over the rasam. Switch off the heat as soon as the rasam foams lightly at the edges.
Rest 2 minutes, then ladle over the cooked grain.

ulundhu-kali-with-buttermilk.name: Ulundhu Kali with Buttermilk
cooking_steps: Bring 200 ml water with salt, cumin, and ginger to a boil over medium heat.
Whisk in the slurry steadily and lower the heat. Cook for 6 to 8 minutes, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens and leaves the sides of the pan.
Switch to low heat and beat in the warm buttermilk a little at a time until smooth and glossy.
Cook 1 final minute, then serve soft with a spoon rather than letting it set fully firm.

vazhaithandu-kootu-with-brown-rice.name: Vazhaithandu Kootu with Brown Rice
cooking_steps: Cook the vegetable with 250 ml water and salt over medium heat for 8 to 10 minutes until just tender.
Add the cooked legume and the coconut paste and simmer on low heat for 5 minutes, stirring often so the base does not catch.
In a small pan, heat oil on medium heat, crackle mustard and urad dal, then add curry leaves. Pour this tempering over the kootu immediately.
Fold once, rest 2 minutes for the flavors to settle, and serve with the cooked grain.

vegetable-appam-with-stew.name: Vegetable Appam with Stew
cooking_steps: Cook the appam one at a time in an appachatti, swirling the batter for lacy edges and a thicker center.
For the stew, soften onion, ginger, chilli, and curry leaves in oil over medium heat for 2 minutes.
Add the vegetable, peas, salt, and 180 ml water; cover and cook on medium-low heat for 7 minutes until almost tender.
Add coconut milk and simmer gently on low heat for 4 minutes. Serve the stew with hot appam.

vegetable-kambu-kozhukattai.name: Vegetable Kambu Kozhukattai
cooking_steps: Heat oil in a saute pan on medium heat. Add mustard seeds and urad dal and cook until the dal is lightly golden. Stir in curry leaves, ginger, chilli, and the vegetable; cook 2 minutes.
Pour in water and salt and bring to a full boil over high heat. Lower the heat and stir in the grain slowly until it forms a soft mass.
Cover and rest off heat for 5 minutes. When cool enough to handle, shape into small oblong dumplings with damp hands.
Steam on medium heat for 10 to 12 minutes until the surface looks matte and firm. Serve warm with chutney or a thin lentil broth.

vegetable-kozhukattai-in-coconut-broth.name: Vegetable Kozhukattai in Coconut Broth
cooking_steps: Steam the shaped dumplings on medium heat for 10 minutes until firm and matte.
In a pot, warm oil and saute ginger, chilli, curry leaves, and the vegetable for 2 minutes on medium heat.
Add cooked moong dal, water, coconut milk, and salt and simmer on low heat for 5 minutes.
Slide in the steamed dumplings and cook 2 minutes more so they absorb some broth before serving.

vegetable-kurma-with-ragi-idiyappam.name: Vegetable Kurma with Ragi Idiyappam
cooking_steps: Warm a pot on medium heat with oil. Add onion, ginger, chilli, and curry leaves and soften for 2 minutes without browning.
Add the vegetable, peas, salt, and 180 ml water. Cover and cook on medium-low heat for 6 minutes until just tender.
Add coconut milk and simmer gently on low heat for 4 minutes until lightly thickened.
Serve the hot stew over the idiyappam so the strands absorb flavor but still retain shape.

vegetable-oats-kanji.name: Vegetable Oats Kanji
cooking_steps: Combine the grain, moong dal, vegetable, salt, and water in a pot and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce to low heat and cook covered for 18 to 20 minutes, stirring twice, until the mixture turns soft and porridge-like.
Heat oil in a small pan, add cumin, pepper, and ginger, and cook on medium heat for 30 seconds. Stir into the kanji.
Cool for 2 minutes, then fold in the buttermilk for a softer finish before serving.

vegetable-quinoa-paniyaram.name: Vegetable Quinoa Paniyaram
cooking_steps: Heat the paniyaram pan over medium heat for 2 minutes and brush each cavity lightly with oil.
Mix onion, vegetable, ginger, chilli, curry leaves, and salt into the batter. Fill each cavity three-quarters full.
Cook covered on medium-low heat for 3 minutes until the edges set and the bottom turns deep golden.
Turn each paniyaram with a skewer, cook 2 more minutes until evenly browned and springy, then serve hot.

vegetable-thakkali-aval.name: Vegetable Thakkali Aval
cooking_steps: Heat a wide pan on medium heat with oil. Add mustard, urad dal, curry leaves, and chilli and cook 45 seconds.
Add onion and vegetable and saute 3 minutes until just tender.
Add the sevai, salt, and 30 ml water and toss on low heat for 2 minutes.
Finish with coconut, toss once, and serve immediately.

Pour a ladle of batter into a thick round, scatter the vegetables over the top, and press gently.
Cook 3 minutes on medium-low heat until the bottom turns golden and the top starts to set.
Flip and cook 1 minute on low heat, then serve with thin sambar.
